title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Failure Case: Portfolio Defense
summary: Ship and maintain a cross-provider prompt system with regression evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt portfolio defense increment in a production prompt portfolio and release gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend prompt design choices with evidence across Claude, Codex, changed cases, and limitations.
Use controlled experiments, canaries, and rollback criteria for prompt changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Portfolio Defense
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Portfolio Defense contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend prompt design choices with evidence across claude, codex, changed cases, and limitations.

content.text: Defend prompt design choices with evidence across Claude, Codex, changed cases, and limitations. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt production capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Portfolio Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production prompt portfolio and release gate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A portfolio of successful screenshots proves transferable prompt skill. This fails the Portfolio Defense contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Portfolio Defense evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-portfolio-defense-1oe631" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Portfolio Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-portfolio-defense-1oe631
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Portfolio Defense model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Portfolio Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend prompt design choices with evidence across Claude, Codex, changed cases, and limitations.
